calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion To Amend Pleadings And To Correct Clerical Error In Clerks Judgment Nunc Pro Tunc
Off Calendar Apr-15-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF DEPARTMENT OF INDUSTRIAL RELATIONS's Motion To Amend Pleadings And To Correct Clerical Error In Clerks Judgment Nunc Pro Tunc.

Non-Party State Labor Commissioner's Motion To Amend Pleadings And To Correct Clerical Error In Clerks Judgment Nunc Pro Tunc is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART.

Good cause appearing, the judgment may be amended nunc pro tunc to March 30, 2020, to reflect the true name of Plaintiff and judgment creditor as Tejendra Pandey. The caption may be corrected to reflect Plaintiff's true name. The court will not "amend" "all pleadings in the case" or order amendment of all pleadings to correct the name. Such relief is not feasible and not appropriate.

Moving Party must present a proposed amended judgment (with caption corrected) to the Clerk of the court, along with this order, in order to secure a new judgment.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
